free_text: reactions on 86Sr, with particular emphasis on the
formation of isomeric states in 86Y and 85Y
JUelich Light Ion Cyclotron  used at 44 MeV
with thickness of 50 mu-m. The netto weight of the
sediments lay in the range of 5 to 7 mg, amounting to
sediment, sandwiched between two Al foils, served as
the target sample. Several such samples were stacked
together with thin circular foils (diameter 13 mm) of
Cu and Ti. In total six stacks, each with five to nine
target samples together with several monitor foils,
were irradiated at LBNL and Julich.
The radioactivity of the irradiated foils was
gamma-ray detectors, supplied by ORTEC.The energy
resolutions at 1332.5 keV of the detectors used were:
1.9 keV at FZJ and 2.5 keV at LBNL.
